def: v.t. To cause the milk to come into the udder of animals ( = panhānā).
more: v. a. To cause the milk to come into the udder of animals. See پنـﮩانا panhānā.

def: v.n. To be refreshed, be revived or restored; to take root, to grow vigorously or luxuriantly (a plant or tree); to thrive, flourish, prosper, luxuriate; to recover, revive, get stronger, look blooming; to pick up flesh (after sickness); to increase in bulk, to get fat.
more: v. n. 1. To recover; revive; come round. Gadar se āj tak nahiṅ panpe . 2. To get stronger; get flesh; get a color. 3. To thrive; prosper; flourish.
v. n. 1. To commence increasing in bulk (a man, tree, &c.). 2. To prosper, to flourish, to thrive, to shoot, to grow, to be refreshed or restored.

def: s.m. = H پناري पनारी panārī, s.f. See panālā; panālī.
s.f. = H پنارا पनारा panārā, s.m. See panālā; panālī.
more: s. f. A drain or pipe by which water runs off from the roof of a house.

def: s.m. The bread-fruit or Jak tree, Artocarpus integrifolia; — a thorn; — a species of serpent.
s.f. A disease, pustular and phlegmonoid inflammation of the skin or external organs; pustules.
s.m. A sort of musical instrument, a small drum or tabor (used to accompany singing); — a species of metre.
s.f. = paṇav, q.v. s.m. A sort of musical instrument, a small drum or tabor (used to accompany singing); — a species of metre.
s.m. For compounds of which pan, 'betel-leaf,' is the first member, see s.v. pan.
s.m. For compounds of which pan, 'water,' is the first member, see s.v. pan.
s. m. A kind of drink made from rice.
adj. Five; equal to, or as good as, five; sharp, astute
more: adj. S. पञ्जन् Pr. panch . Five. Sharp; clever; sharp-witted. Phr.—Cunning as a fox; sharp as a needle. Saut merī pāṅch hai, maiṅ us se chhattīsī sivā, Woh to hai do chār meṅ, maiṅ ek hūṅ das bīs meṅ . Wom. Jān Sāhab. If my co-wife has her wits five, I've over six and thirty, She is but one 'mong two to four, I'm one in ten to twenty. pāṅch indrī , n. f. The five senses (A. havās khamsah ). Pāṅchoṅindrī bas kar rākho, ek se dhiyān lagāvo re . Bhajan. Subdue your senses five and fix your thoughts on Him alone.
adj. Five. پانچ سات pānch-sāt, s. Perplexity.
subs: s.m. Toll of five chaṭāṅk in a rupee's worth of rice or paddy (the toll is levied in large cities to defray the expenses of the inspectors of weighmen stationed in the bazars to prevent fraud in the weight and measure of commodities sold therein)
s.f. lit. 'Fives and sevens'; sixes and sevens, confusion, perplexity
(paṅća karma), s.m. The five actions of the body, viz. 1˚ Vomiting; 2˚ Evacuation by bleeding; 3˚ Evacuation by stool; 4˚ Diseased excretion by fæces or purging; 5˚ Blowing the nose
adj. The five, all five; — pāṅćoṅ bhāʼī, s.m. The five Pānḍav princes, viz. Yudhishṭhir, Bhīm-sen, Arjun, Nakul, and Sahdeo
(paṅća-bhūtātmā), s.m. lit. 'Consisting of the five primary elements'; a human being

s.m. The five articles of clothing, viz., 1˚ the pagṛī or turban; 2˚ the angarkhā or coat; 3˚ the pāʼe-jāma or trousers; 4˚ the du-paṭṭā or sheet or mantle; and 5˚ the rūmāl, or handkerchief; a complete suit of clothes
s.m. The five arms or weapons, viz. 1˚ the ḍhāl or shield; 2˚ the talwār or sword; 3˚ the barćhī or spear; 4˚ the tīr or arrow; and 5˚ the kamān or bow.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi पंच (paṃca), पांच (pāṃca), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀁𑀘 (paṃca), from Sanskrit पञ्च (páñca).

def: s.m. Any collection or aggregate of five (see panjā and panja); a five-pronged fork (used in winnowing corn).

adj. & s.m. Five; — an assembly of (originally) five men, assembly, meeting, council; a body of arbitrators, court of arbitration; a jury; — a member of a court of arbitration, arbitrator, umpire, judge; — the head man of a caste; the head man of a village; — panćoṅ, panćau (and in Braj), panćan, s.m. pl. (of panć), Associates, companions, friends
more: adj. Five. panch-ādhyā , n. f. The five chapters of the Sri-Bhāgwat , comprising a detail of the sports of Krishna with the Gopis. panch-āgnī ; Tir. pachāg , n. m. The five fires amidst which the devotee performs penance during the summer season, or four fires lighted severally to the north, south, east, and west, with the sun overhead. panch-āmrit , n. m. Milk, curds, sugar, ghī , and honey. A mixture of. panch-āmlā , n. m. A composition of five acid juices, viz. the jujube, pomegranate, sorrel, spondias, and citron. panch-indrī , n. m. The five organs of sense, viz. the eye, ear, nose, tongue, and skin; or those of action, as the hands, feet, windpipe, anus , and the organ of generation. panch-āṅg , n. m. 1. Five modes of devotion, viz. silent prayer, burnt offering, libations, bathing idols, and feeding Brahmins. panch-bhadrā , n. m. 1. A horse with five good marks. 2. A sauce of five…

n. m. 1. An assembly of five men; a jury of arbitrators; a village council; an umpire. 1. Panch kaheṅ billī, to billī hī sahī . Prov. If the panch (corporation) say it is a cat, a cat let it be. 2. Panch jahāṅ Parmeshar . Or, Panch mil Khudā mil . Prov. Where five men agree 'tis God's decree. 3. Pāṅch panch mil kīje kāj, hāre jīte āve na lāj . Prov. Whate'er you have to do in Council do, Succeed or fail, no shame will rest on you. 4. Panchoṅ kī dī-huī hai . 2. A village head man; the head man of a caste. 3. An arbitrator; judge; assessor. panch faislah , n. m. Judgment by arbitration; the written award of a court of arbitration. panchoṅ kā piyālā , n. m. (Slang.) A hukka. sar-panch , n. m. A head assessor or judge; a president; chairman.
n. f. A bowstring. Bhirkuṭī dhanush chaṛhāë anjan barnī panach-ke, Lochan bān chalāë, māre par jīwat raheṅ . Premsāgar.

1. adj. Five. 2. s. m. A council, an assembly, a meeting, a company. 3. Arbitrators. پنچ پلو पंचपल्लव panch pallav, The branches of five trees which are used in religious ceremonies. پنچ شاکها पंचशाखा panch-shākhā (Pers. پنج شاخه) see پنجه panja, sign. 2. پنچ پريا panch-piriyā, 1. Any person, Hindū or other, who worships the five pīrs or saints of the Musalmāns. 2. A cast of ḥalāl-ḵẖors. پنچ پاتر पंचपात्र panch-pātr, s. m. A kind of vessel used by Hindūs during worship.
subs: (paṅća+adh˚), s.m. Five readings, sections, or chapters
panćādhyā, s.f. The aggregate of five chapters of the Bhāgavat Purāṇ, detailing the sports of Kr̤ishṇa with the milkmaids

(panća+agni), s.m. A collection of five fires amidst which certain Hindū devotees perform penance during the hot season (viz. one fire placed respectively east, west, north, and south of them, the sun overhead being the fifth); — five mystic fires supposed to be present in the body
(panća+am˚), s.m. A mixture consisting of milk, curds, sugar, ghī, and honey; — the aggregate of any five drugs of supposed efficacy
(˚ća+ām˚), s.m. The aggregate of five acid plants, viz. the jujube, pomegranate, sorrel, spondias, and citron

(˚ća+an˚), adj. & s.m. Five-limbed, five-membered; having five parts or subdivisions; — five limbs or members of the body; five modes of devotion (viz. silent prayer, burnt offering, libations, bathing idols, and feeding Brahmans); — a calendar or almanac treating of five things, viz. solar days, lunar days, the periods of asterisms, Yogas, and Karaṇas; — reverence or obeisance made with the arms, knees, head, voice, and look
(˚ća+ān˚), adj. & s.m. Five-faced; an epithet of Śiva; a lion (often used at the end of names of learned men to express respect); — panćānanī, s.f. An epithet of Durgā, the spouse of Śiva
s.m. A fine equal to the fifth part of anything lost or stolen
adj. & s.m. Having five good qualities; — a sauce or condiment of five good ingredients; a horse with five auspicious marks or spots (viz. on the chest, back, face, and flanks)
s.m. The five elements, earth, air, fire, water, and aether or space; — panć-bhūt-ātmā, adj. & s.m. Consisting, or formed of, the five primary elements; — an epithet of any individual human being
s.m. Five cups or vessels collectively; — a śrāddh in which offerings are made in five vessels; — a small vessel with which water is poured over the idol while worshipping
s.m. The five vital airs of the body
s.f. A species of small shrub
s.m. One who (whether Hindū or Mohammadan) worships the five saints (pīr) of the Mohammadans
s.m. The aggregate of five sprigs or young shoots of the spondias, rose-apple, bel, citron, and wood-apple; — a medical preparation from the same
s.m. The five kinds of animals allowed to be killed and eaten, viz. the hare, the porcupine, the alligator, the rhinoceros, and the tortoise
s.m. The five pīrs or Mohammadan saints (worshipped by most Hindūs as well as Musalmāns)
s.m. An ascetic who in the hot weather sits between four fires placed towards the four quarters with the burning sun above (cf. panćāgni)

vulg. panć-tat, s.m. The five elements ( = panć-bhūt, q.v.); — the five essentials of certain rites (all beginning with ma, viz.), madya, 'wine,' māṅs, 'meat,' matsya, 'fish,' mudrā, 'mystic intertwining of the fingers,' and maithun, 'sexual intercourse'
vulg. panć-tīrath, s.f. Any five chief places of pilgrimage; — bathing on the day of the Equinox
panć-janya, s.m. The five kinds or classes of beings, viz. gods, men, Gandharvas and Apsaras, serpents, and manes
s.f. An assemblage or aggregate of five persons
adv. In five ways; in five parts; five-fold
adj. & s.m. Lasting five nights or days; — a period of five nights; — name of a sacrifice which lasts five days; — a general term for the sacred books of various Vaiśnav sects
adj. & s.m. Relating to the five ratios or proportions of numbers; — the rule of five, double rule of three
s.m. A collection of five jewels or precious things, viz. gold, the diamond, sapphire, ruby, and pearl
paṅć-rasī, adj. Composed of five metals or fluids
s.m. Collection of five kinds of aromatic vegetable substances, viz. cloves, nutmeg, camphor, aloe wood, and kakkolā
s.m. The five things in a house by which animal life may be accidentally destroyed, viz. the fire-place, the curry-slab, the broom, the pestle and mortar, and the water-pot
paṅć-śākhā, adj. & s.m. Having five branches; — the hand
s.m. Five kinds of sounds; — five kinds of musical instruments
adj. & s.m. Having five arrows; a name of Kāma, the god of love
s.m. Judgment by arbitration; — the award of a court of arbitration
s.m. The five actions of the body; the five kinds of treatment, viz. giving emetics, purging, giving sternutatories, and administering enemas of two kinds, oily and not oily
s.f. (in Myth.) The five virgins, viz. Tārā, Kuntī, Ahalyā, Mandodarī, and Sītā
vulg. paṅć-kosī, s.f. The aggregate of five kos; — the pilgrimage road round the city of Benares

s.m. The five sheaths or cases supposed to invest the soul (viz. the gross form, or the form supported by food, the organs of action, the organs of perception including the mind, the intellect, the elements of identity and divine wisdom)
s.m. The five spices, viz. long pepper, its root, Chai, or Piper Chaba, plumbago, and dry ginger
paṅć-khanā, adj. Consisting of five floors, stories, or apartments (a building), five-storied, &c.
s.m. Five morsels of food taken to the mouth by the hand
s.m. Five products of the cow, viz. milk, curds, butter, urine, and cow-dung
s.m. = paṅć-laṛī, s.f. 'Consisting of five strings or rows'; a necklace of five strings or rows

adj. & s.m. Possessing five characteristics; — an epithet of a purāṇa or mythological poem (which ought strictly to comprehend five topics, viz. the creation of the universe, its destruction and renovation, the genealogy of gods and patriarchs, the reigns of the Manus, and the history of the solar and lunar races)
s.m. A metallic alloy containing five metals, viz. copper, brass, tin, lead, and iron
adj. & s.m. Happening every five months, five-monthly; containing five months; — the koʼil or Indian cuckoo
(-ko), To regard (one) as an arbitrator, to constitute (one) an umpire
s.m. The five essentials of the left hand, viz. wine, man's flesh, fish, copulation, and gesticulation

s.m. paṅć-mūlī, s.f. A collection or group of five roots or plants with tuberous roots, viz. Bel, Premna longifolia, Cassia, Gmelina arborea, and the trumpet-flower; — or Hedysarum gangeticum, Hedysarum lagopodioides, Solanum melongena, Solanum jacquini, and Tribulus lanuginosus

s.m. The five great sacrifices of the Hindūs, viz. the worship of spirits (by offerings of perfumes and flowers), the worship of progenitors (by obsequial rites), the worship of gods (by oblations with fire), the worship of the Vedas (by studying them), and the worship of mankind (by hospitality)
adj. Of five stories, five-storied (a building)
adj. & s.m. Having five rivers; — an epithet of the Panjāb (q.v.s.v. panj)
adj. & s.m. Five-clawed, having five nails; — any animal with five claws or toes, e.g. elephant, tiger, tortoise, &c.
(paṅća+uttara), s.m. A transit duty of five per cent.; a deduction from rent, &c. of five per cent.; a toll-house, custom-house
(˚ća+in˚), s.m. The five organs of sense (the eye, ear, nose, tongue, and skin); the five organs of action (the hand, foot, larynx, anus, and the organ of generation).

def: adj. For compounds of which panća or panć is the first member, see s.v. panća.

adj. & s.m. Relating to five; consisting or made up of five; bought with five, &c.; taking five per cent.; — the number five; any collection or aggregate of five; a five; — taxes levied by the zamīndārs over and above the fixed revenue.
more: n. m. See پچکها panch-kosī , n. The pilgrimage round Benaras. panch-kon , A. mukhammas; A pentagon. panch-gavyā , n. m. The five articles yielded by the cow, viz. milk, curd, clarified butter, dung, and urine. panch-mahā-yagyā , n. The five great sacraments of the Hindus, viz. the worship of spirits (by offerings of perfumes and flowers), of progenitors (by obsequial rites), of gods (by sacrificial fires), of Veds (by studying them), and of mankind (by hospitality).

def: s.f. A bow-string ( = panać, q.v.) s.f. A bow-string.

adj. & s.m. Relating or belonging to the Paṅćālas, dwelling in or ruling over the country of the Paṅćālas; — sovereign or prince of the Paṅćālas; the country of the Paṅćālas; — an association of five guilds, viz. those of the carpenter, weaver, barber, washerman, and shoemaker.
more: n. m. pan five, chāl occupation. The association of five trades, viz . carpenter, weaver, barber, washerman, and shoe maker.

def: s.m. Name of a warrior-tribe and of their country in the north of India; name of a prince of that tribe.
more: adj. (dakh.) Artful, cunning, deceitful: see مکار makkār.

def: s.m. = pāṅćāl, q.v. adj. & s.m. Relating or belonging to the Paṅćālas, dwelling in or ruling over the country of the Paṅćālas; — sovereign or prince of the Paṅćālas; the country of the Paṅćālas; — an association of five guilds, viz. those of the carpenter, weaver, barber, washerman, and shoemaker.
adj. Dexterous, clever, artful, cunning, deceitful.

adj. & s.m. Fifth; — the fifth or (in later times) the seventh note of the Hindū musical scale (so called because, according to the Hindūs, its tone is produced by air drawn from five parts of the body, the navel, breast, throat, heart, and forehead); — one of the rāgs or musical modes.
more: n. m. 1. A rāg or sur (a note in music). 2. The soprano .
1. adj. Fifth. 2. s. m. Name of a rāg and of a sur or musical note.

def: s.f. The fifth day of the half month or of the lunar fortnight.
more: The fifth day of the lunar fortnight.
s. f. The fifth day of the lunar fortnight.

def: adj. Five ( = panća).
s.m. (Braj pl. of panć), Associates, companions, friends ( = panćoṅ).
adj. Ninety-five ( = paćānwe, q.v.).
adj. Fifty ( = paćās, q.v.). adj. Fifty.
s.f. Five-foldness, five-fold state; collection or aggregate of five things; — separation into the five elements of which the body consists, dissolution, death.
s.m. A fee or duty of five percent. levied on a successful litigant.
s.m. = panćatā.
adj. Fifty-five ( = paćpan, q.v.). adj. Fifty-five
subs: s.m. The rule under which a Government servant (in India) is compelled to retire from service on attaining his fifty-fifth year of age.

def: s.f. A meeting of any particular society (generally as a court of inquiry), a court of arbitration (consisting of five or more members); a body of arbitrators; a jury, inquest, committee, council ( = panć); — the award of a court of arbitration; — panćāyatī, s.f. Relationship, affinity
more: n. f. 1. A native court of arbitration consisting of five or more members; a meeting; a commission; a committee; a council. 2. An arbitration award. 3. ( Ironic .) Jabbering; nonsense. panchā'yat-khāngī , Ped. for H. gharelū panchāyat , n. f. Private arbitration; a settlement of family quarrels by relations. panchāyat sarkārī , n. f. A court of arbitration appointed by the public authorities. panchāyat karnā yā joṛnā , v. a. 1. To form a court of arbitration. 2. To send for the members of a panchāyat or private council to sit as judges. panchāyat-nāmah , n. m. The written award of a court of arbitration. jātī panchāyat , n. f. An arbitration by persons of the same caste as the litigant parties. panchā'ëtī , adj. 1. Belonging to a panchāyat . 2. Common; public; open to all. Panchāëtī makān . A public building or meeting hall. 3. Just; true; right. Panchaëtī bāt kaho . 4. Illegitimate; bas…
s. f. A meeting of any particular society (generally, as a court of inquiry); a jury, an inquest.
subs: (or panćāyatī) jātī, or jātī panćāyat, s.f. An arbitration by persons of the same caste as the litigant parties
(or panćāyatī) ḵẖāngī, s.f. Private arbitration; settlement of family quarrels by relations
(or paṅćāyatī) sarkārī, s.f. A court of arbitration appointed by public authority
v.n. To form a court of arbitration; to assemble the members of a panćāyat to sit as umpires
s.m. The written award of a court of arbitration.

def: adj. Of or belonging to a panćāyat; for the meeting of a panćāyat, public, common (as a building, &c.); — equitable, just, right, proper; — illegitimate, bastard
subs: s.m. lit. 'A common, or everybody's brother-in-law,' a term of abuse.

def: s.f. A meeting of any particular society (generally as a court of inquiry), a court of arbitration (consisting of five or more members); a body of arbitrators; a jury, inquest, committee, council ( = panć); — the award of a court of arbitration; — panćāyatī, s.f. Relationship, affinity
more: s. f. 1. Same as پنچايت panchāyat, q. v. 2. Relationship.
subs: (or panćāyatī) jātī, or jātī panćāyat, s.f. An arbitration by persons of the same caste as the litigant parties

def: s.m. Running or discharge from a sore, &c.
adj. Fifteenth.
s. f. The aggregate of five chapters of the Srī Bhāgavat, comprising a detail of the sports of Kr̤ish̤ṇa with the Gopīs.
s. Five fires (amidst which a devotee performs penance during the summer season; or four fires lighted severally to the north, south, east, and west, and the sun over head).
adj. Consisting of five floors or stories (a house, &c.).
s. f. A watermill.
s.m. The tail of a paper kite; — a follower; a parasite; one who dogs the steps of another (esp. of a woman).
more: n. m. S. पुच्छ a tail. 1. The tail of a paper kite. 2. A follower; retainer. 3. A parasite; tuft hunter; one who follows a woman like a dog. Jisko jāne hai kuchh ṭakoṅ-wālā, Us ke pīchhe hai āp panchhālā . Saudā. Where'er a moneyed man he spies He follows as his tail his eyes.
s. m. The tail of a paper kite.

def: s. f. Artifice, cunning, &c.
s. f. The fifth day of the lunar fortnight.
s. m. 1. A name of Siva. 2. A lion.
s. The five elements or principles according to the Hindūs, namely, earth, water, fire, air, and space or ether.
s. Associates, friends.
adj. Twenty five.
adj. Twenty fifth.
adj. Of five years.
s.f. A bird
more: n. m. S. पक्षी from पक्ष a wing. 1. A bird. 1. Panchhī kahe pukār-ke 'suni leho biādhā bāt,' 'Ek din aisā āī paṛī, jī, ham tum jaivo sāth.' Bhoj . Song. The feathered tribe cry out, 'O listen, fowler, pray, Together we shall stand upon the judgment day.' 2. Phaṅsā jāl meṅ ā-ke panchhī-dānā panī bhūl gayā . Song. The poor bird in a net was caught, both food and drink by him forgot. 2. ( Met .) A man; fellow; individual.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit पक्षिन् (pakṣín, “bird”, literally “winged”). First attested as Old Hindi पंषी (pãṣī). Doublet of پَکْشِی (pakśī).

def: adj. Consisting of five floors or stories (a house, &c.).
adj. Of five rows or strings (a necklace, &c.).
s. f. A necklace of five rows or strings.

adj. All five. pāṅchoṅ kapṛe , n. m. The five articles of clothing. viz. pagṛī turban, aṅgarkhā coat, pājāmā treusers; ḍu-paṭṭā sheet, and rumāl handkerchief; a complete suit. Pāṅchoṅ kapṛe utār liye, aur chum naṅgā kar-ke pāṅchoṅ hatyār , n. m. The five arms or weapons. viz. ḍhāl a shield, talvār a sword, barchhī a spear, tīr an arrow, and kamān a bow. Ghoṛā lo ghuṛ-sāl se, aur pāṅchoṅ lo hatyār, Dil chāhe jahāṅ jāīye, kuchh mat karo bichār . Song. Sarwan Nīr.

adj. S. पंचम, Pr. panchamo . Fifth. pāṅch'vāṅ sawār honā, lit . To be the fifth horseman. To put in one's own small claim, as one should say 'I shot a tiger too.' ☞ A potter, riding on his ass, behind four horsemen bound for the Deccan, being asked by a traveller where the horsemen were going, answered, 'We five horsemen are going to the Deccan.' Tā ke mashhūr hoṅ hazāroṅ meṅ, Ham bhi haiṅ pāṅchweṅ sawāroṅ meṅ . Shauq.
s. f. The fifth day of the moon or of the half month.
s.m. pl. See s.v. panć
subs: also = pāṅćoṅ. adj. Five; equal to
or as good as, five; sharp, astute

s.f. lit. 'Fives and sevens'; sixes and sevens, confusion, perplexity

adj. The five, all five; — pāṅćoṅ bhāʼī, s.m. The five Pānḍav princes, viz. Yudhishṭhir, Bhīm-sen, Arjun, Nakul, and Sahdeo
(paṅća-bhūtātmā), s.m. lit. 'Consisting of the five primary elements'; a human being

def: s.m. See s.v. pan, 'water.' s.m. Drinking; drink, beverage
more: s. m. An earthen vessel with a narrow neck and a hole in the bottom. When filled with water and the mouth stopped, no water flows from the hole below till the hand or whatever stopped the mouth be removed. (It is used as an experiment to illustrate the doctrine of the horror vacui by the followers of Aristotle).
subs: v.n. To take a drink, to drink; to refresh oneself, make a repast (usually with jal, e.g. jal-pān kar-lo).

def: s.m. See s.v. panća. adj. & s.m. Five; — an assembly of (originally) five men, assembly, meeting, council; a body of arbitrators, court of arbitration; a jury; — a member of a court of arbitration, arbitrator, umpire, judge; — the head man of a caste; the head man of a village; — panćoṅ, panćau (and in Braj), panćan, s.m. pl. (of panć), Associates, companions, friends
subs: (paṅća+adh˚), s.m. Five readings, sections, or chapters
panćādhyā, s.f. The aggregate of five chapters of the Bhāgavat Purāṇ, detailing the sports of Kr̤ishṇa with the milkmaids

(panća+agni), s.m. A collection of five fires amidst which certain Hindū devotees perform penance during the hot season (viz. one fire placed respectively east, west, north, and south of them, the sun overhead being the fifth); — five mystic fires supposed to be present in the body
(panća+am˚), s.m. A mixture consisting of milk, curds, sugar, ghī, and honey; — the aggregate of any five drugs of supposed efficacy
(˚ća+ām˚), s.m. The aggregate of five acid plants, viz. the jujube, pomegranate, sorrel, spondias, and citron

(˚ća+an˚), adj. & s.m. Five-limbed, five-membered; having five parts or subdivisions; — five limbs or members of the body; five modes of devotion (viz. silent prayer, burnt offering, libations, bathing idols, and feeding Brahmans); — a calendar or almanac treating of five things, viz. solar days, lunar days, the periods of asterisms, Yogas, and Karaṇas; — reverence or obeisance made with the arms, knees, head, voice, and look
(˚ća+ān˚), adj. & s.m. Five-faced; an epithet of Śiva; a lion (often used at the end of names of learned men to express respect); — panćānanī, s.f. An epithet of Durgā, the spouse of Śiva
s.m. A fine equal to the fifth part of anything lost or stolen
adj. & s.m. Having five good qualities; — a sauce or condiment of five good ingredients; a horse with five auspicious marks or spots (viz. on the chest, back, face, and flanks)
s.m. The five elements, earth, air, fire, water, and aether or space; — panć-bhūt-ātmā, adj. & s.m. Consisting, or formed of, the five primary elements; — an epithet of any individual human being
s.m. Five cups or vessels collectively; — a śrāddh in which offerings are made in five vessels; — a small vessel with which water is poured over the idol while worshipping
s.m. The five vital airs of the body
s.f. A species of small shrub
s.m. One who (whether Hindū or Mohammadan) worships the five saints (pīr) of the Mohammadans
s.m. The aggregate of five sprigs or young shoots of the spondias, rose-apple, bel, citron, and wood-apple; — a medical preparation from the same
s.m. The five kinds of animals allowed to be killed and eaten, viz. the hare, the porcupine, the alligator, the rhinoceros, and the tortoise
s.m. The five pīrs or Mohammadan saints (worshipped by most Hindūs as well as Musalmāns)
s.m. An ascetic who in the hot weather sits between four fires placed towards the four quarters with the burning sun above (cf. panćāgni)

vulg. panć-tat, s.m. The five elements ( = panć-bhūt, q.v.); — the five essentials of certain rites (all beginning with ma, viz.), madya, 'wine,' māṅs, 'meat,' matsya, 'fish,' mudrā, 'mystic intertwining of the fingers,' and maithun, 'sexual intercourse'
vulg. panć-tīrath, s.f. Any five chief places of pilgrimage; — bathing on the day of the Equinox
panć-janya, s.m. The five kinds or classes of beings, viz. gods, men, Gandharvas and Apsaras, serpents, and manes
s.f. An assemblage or aggregate of five persons
adv. In five ways; in five parts; five-fold
adj. & s.m. Lasting five nights or days; — a period of five nights; — name of a sacrifice which lasts five days; — a general term for the sacred books of various Vaiśnav sects
adj. & s.m. Relating to the five ratios or proportions of numbers; — the rule of five, double rule of three
s.m. A collection of five jewels or precious things, viz. gold, the diamond, sapphire, ruby, and pearl
paṅć-rasī, adj. Composed of five metals or fluids
s.m. Collection of five kinds of aromatic vegetable substances, viz. cloves, nutmeg, camphor, aloe wood, and kakkolā
s.m. The five things in a house by which animal life may be accidentally destroyed, viz. the fire-place, the curry-slab, the broom, the pestle and mortar, and the water-pot
paṅć-śākhā, adj. & s.m. Having five branches; — the hand
s.m. Five kinds of sounds; — five kinds of musical instruments
adj. & s.m. Having five arrows; a name of Kāma, the god of love
s.m. Judgment by arbitration; — the award of a court of arbitration
s.m. The five actions of the body; the five kinds of treatment, viz. giving emetics, purging, giving sternutatories, and administering enemas of two kinds, oily and not oily
s.f. (in Myth.) The five virgins, viz. Tārā, Kuntī, Ahalyā, Mandodarī, and Sītā
vulg. paṅć-kosī, s.f. The aggregate of five kos; — the pilgrimage road round the city of Benares

s.m. The five sheaths or cases supposed to invest the soul (viz. the gross form, or the form supported by food, the organs of action, the organs of perception including the mind, the intellect, the elements of identity and divine wisdom)
s.m. The five spices, viz. long pepper, its root, Chai, or Piper Chaba, plumbago, and dry ginger
paṅć-khanā, adj. Consisting of five floors, stories, or apartments (a building), five-storied, &c.
s.m. Five morsels of food taken to the mouth by the hand
s.m. Five products of the cow, viz. milk, curds, butter, urine, and cow-dung
s.m. = paṅć-laṛī, s.f. 'Consisting of five strings or rows'; a necklace of five strings or rows

adj. & s.m. Possessing five characteristics; — an epithet of a purāṇa or mythological poem (which ought strictly to comprehend five topics, viz. the creation of the universe, its destruction and renovation, the genealogy of gods and patriarchs, the reigns of the Manus, and the history of the solar and lunar races)
s.m. A metallic alloy containing five metals, viz. copper, brass, tin, lead, and iron
adj. & s.m. Happening every five months, five-monthly; containing five months; — the koʼil or Indian cuckoo
(-ko), To regard (one) as an arbitrator, to constitute (one) an umpire
s.m. The five essentials of the left hand, viz. wine, man's flesh, fish, copulation, and gesticulation

s.m. paṅć-mūlī, s.f. A collection or group of five roots or plants with tuberous roots, viz. Bel, Premna longifolia, Cassia, Gmelina arborea, and the trumpet-flower; — or Hedysarum gangeticum, Hedysarum lagopodioides, Solanum melongena, Solanum jacquini, and Tribulus lanuginosus

s.m. The five great sacrifices of the Hindūs, viz. the worship of spirits (by offerings of perfumes and flowers), the worship of progenitors (by obsequial rites), the worship of gods (by oblations with fire), the worship of the Vedas (by studying them), and the worship of mankind (by hospitality)
adj. Of five stories, five-storied (a building)
adj. & s.m. Having five rivers; — an epithet of the Panjāb (q.v.s.v. panj)
adj. & s.m. Five-clawed, having five nails; — any animal with five claws or toes, e.g. elephant, tiger, tortoise, &c.
(paṅća+uttara), s.m. A transit duty of five per cent.; a deduction from rent, &c. of five per cent.; a toll-house, custom-house
(˚ća+in˚), s.m. The five organs of sense (the eye, ear, nose, tongue, and skin); the five organs of action (the hand, foot, larynx, anus, and the organ of generation).

def: s.f. A species of metre.
s.f. The fifth day of the moon, or of the half month
subs: adv. Fifthly, in the fifth place.

def: s.f. A woman who has no breasts or milk.
s.m. Advice, counsel, admonition; moral maxim
more: n. m. S. पिण्ड to roll into a lump or ball. 1. A lump (of flour); a funeral cake. A ball of rice or meat, especially that which is offered at obsequial rites or Sharādhs to deceased parents and progenitors, or to the manes in general. 1. Maṅgnī ke satuā, sasuā ke pinḍ . Prov. For offering to make to her mother-in-law's manes Of grain-meal she borrowed but just a few grains. 2. Jaëgo munḍ, taëgo pinḍ . Prov. As many heads (sons) so many offerings to the manes. 2. The body; person ( badan ). pinḍ-adhikārī , n. m. The legal presenter of the funeral cakes, usually the nearest relatives in the male line. pinḍ-dān , n. Offering of the funeral cakes made of barley. pinḍ paṛnā , v. n. To follow; pursue; to be bent on. pinḍ chhuṭnā , v. n. To be rid of. pinḍ chhuṛānā , v. n. To be quit of (debt, evil). pinḍ-rog , n. m. 1. Wasting of the body; merasmus. Pī kāran pīrī bhaī, log kaheṅ pinḍ-rog . Pro…
s. m. Advice, admonition, counsel. پندگو pand-go, Adviser, monitor.
subs: s.m. One who takes or listens to advice

def: s.m. = panḍā, q.v.
s.f. Wisdom, intelligence, understanding; science, learning.
more: (perhaps, for पंडित ) s. m. A minister who presides at the temple of an idol.

def: s.m. A minister or priest (a Brāhman) who presides at the temple of an idol (the office is hereditary; and in some places, as at Benares, the Panḍā officiates only on special occasions, the duties of the daily worship being performed by subordinate priests or pujārīs in his employ).
s.m. An impotent person, eunuch; a catamite.
more: s. m. Name of a sovereign of ancient Delhi, and nominal father of Yudhish̤ṭhir and the other four Pānḍava princes.

s. m. 1. Body, person. پنڐپڙنا pinḍ paṛnā, To follow, to pursue, to be intent on. پنڐچهڙانا pinḍ chhuṛānā, To avoid, to escape. 2. Balls made of flour or rice, at a religious ceremony of Hindūs. (They are afterwards thrown into the river or given to cows).

def: s.m. Eunuch, &c. = panḍa, q.v.
s. m. A box for holding betel and its apparatus.
adj. & s.m. Whitish-yellow, whitish, white; — the colour pale or yellowish white; the many-flowered jasmine, Jasminum elongatum: name of a Nāga; name of a mountain
subs: s.f. A species of plant (com. called sītalā).

def: = H پندرا पंदरा pandrā, Corr. of pandrah, q.v. (used in comp.)
s.f. Whitish, or chalky, earth or soil.
adj. = pandrah-wāṅ, q.v. adj. (f. wīṅ), Fifteenth; — pandrah-wīṅ, s.f. The fifteenth day of the month; the fifteenth day of a half month, the full or the new moon.
more: adj. (dakh.) Fifteenth ( Harris ): see پندرهوان pandrah-wāṅ.

def: s. (dakh.) A fifteen days or a fortnight.
s.m. A fortnight, fifteen days.
adj. & s.m. Belonging to the sons of Pānḍu; connected with the five Pānḍavas; — (patronym.) a son or descendant of Pāṇḍu; a partisan of the sons of Pānḍu.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit पाण्डव (pāṇḍava).

def: s.m. pl. The five Pānḍav princes.

n. m. Cont. of panḍit . 1. A learned man; a scholar; a teacher; school-master. 2. The title of a branch of the Bharaduājgotra; one of the chief branches of the Kanauj Brahmins. 1. Pāṅḍe jī pachhtāëṅge, aur wohī chane kī khāëṅge . Prov. His reverence regret will feel, too glad to eat the same gram meal. 2. Donoṅ dīn se gaë pāṅḍe, halwā milā na māṅḍe . Prov. From both societies the priest expelled, from Moslem's halvā (sweetmeat) and from Hindū's buttered bread. (Greed is a loser).
s.m. Learned man, scholar; teacher, schoolmaster; a title of Brāhmaṇs (cf. pāṅḍā, panḍā).
more: s. m. A title of Brāhmans; a schoolmaster.

def: adj. = pandrah-wāṅ, q.v. adj. (f. wīṅ), Fifteenth; — pandrah-wīṅ, s.f. The fifteenth day of the month; the fifteenth day of a half month, the full or the new moon.
s.m. = pandarwāṛā, q.v. s.m. A fortnight, fifteen days.
v.t. To coax, wheedle, &c. = phandlānā, q.v. v.t. To take in a noose, snare, or trap; to entrap, ensnare; to entangle; to inveigle; to beguile, divert ( = phuslānā).
s.f. The fifteenth day of a month.

adj. & s.m. Learned, wise; shrewd, skilful, clever, educated; — scholar, learned or wise man, learned Brāhmaṇ; teacher, master, professor, doctor, philosopher, Pandit; a title of respect to Hindūs who are learned in the Brahmanical theology
more: adj. Wise, learned; s. m. A learned Brāhman.
subs: s.m. One who esteems himself a scholar, an ignorant pedant.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit पण्डित (paṇḍita). Doublet of پَنْڈا (panḍā).

def: n. m. 1. A learned man; master; teacher. Panḍit bhaë to kyā bhaë, gale lapeṭe sūt? Bhāo bhakt jāne nahīṅ, bhaë jangal ke bhūt . To be a Pandit naught avails, nor thread about the neck, Who worship true knows not a jot a wild wood demon reck. 2. An honorary title signifying doctor or professor. 3. A Hindu law officer; jurist. panḍat-khānā , n. m. Cor. of bandī-khānā . 1. A jail ( jel-khānā ); a prison ( qaid-khānā ). 2. A gambling house ( juë-khānā ).
s.f. The learning, or the status, of a panḍit; learning, scholarship, knowledge, wisdom; pedantry, display of learning
subs: v.n. To make a display of learning.

def: n. f. 1. The learning of a Brahman; learning. 2. Scholarship; pedantry. 3. The office of a pandit.
s. f. The learning of a Brāhman, learning.
s.f. = panḍitānī, q.v. s.f. The wife of a panḍit; teacher, school-mistress.
s.f. = panḍitānī, q.v. s.f. The wife of a panḍit; teacher, school-mistress.
s.f. The wife of a panḍit; teacher, school-mistress.
s.f. = S پانڐتيه पाण्डित्य pānḍitya, s.m. Scholarship, erudition, learning, wisdom, prudence.
s.m. Prison, gaol; — a gambling house.
s.m. = H پانڐتي पाण्डिती pānḍitī, s.f. Scholarship, erudition, learning, wisdom, prudence.
s.f. The wife of a panḍā or priest; — the wife of a pānḍe.
more: s. f. The wife of a پانڐي.

def: s.m. A species of sugar-cane (cf. pauṅḍā).
= H پندر पंदर pandar, Corr. of pandrah, q.v. (used in comp.)
more: adj. (dakh.) Fifteen ( Harris ): see پندره pandrah.

def: adj. Fifteen.
more: adj. S. पञ्चदश Pr. panrah . Fifteen. pandh'ras , n. m. The fifteenth day of the month. pandharvā'ṛā , n. m. Fifteen days; a fortnight. pandhar'vāṅ, pandrvāṅ, pandhrvīṅ , adj. Fifteenth.
wety: Inherited from Apabhramsa पण्णरह (paṇṇaraha), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀡𑁆𑀡𑀭𑀲 (paṇṇarasa), from Sanskrit पञ्चदश (páñcadaśa).

def: adj. (f. wīṅ), Fifteenth; — pandrah-wīṅ, s.f. The fifteenth day of the month; the fifteenth day of a half month, the full or the new moon.
s. f. The fifteenth day of a half month, full or new moon.

adj. & s.m. Yellowish white, white, pale; pale or yellowish white colour; light-coloured soil, a mixture of clay and sand; land dependent on rain-water; — name of a sovereign of Hastināpur (ancient Dehli), and nominal father of Yudhishṭhir and the other four Pānḍav princes who are distinguished in the great wars related in the Mahābhārat; — s.f. The plant Glycine debilis (com. called mashāni)
subs: s.m.f. Light-coloured soil; a country with a light-coloured soil
pānḍu-bhūm, s.f. The land of the Pānḍavs, the city of Hastināpur
s.m. A son of Pānḍu, an epithet of any one of the Pānḍav princes
s.m. White woollen covering or blanket; a kind of stone (limestone or marble)
s.f. The housings of a royal elephant; a carriage covered with a sort of blanket. — pānḍu-lomā, s.f. The plant Glycine debilis
s.f. Pale or light-coloured soil, chalky soil; — the opal; — pānḍu-nāg, s.m. The plant Rottleria tinctoria

more: s. m. Descendant of Rājā Pānḍu, especially applied to Yudhish̤ṭhira and his four brothers.

pānḍu-bhūm, s.f. The land of the Pānḍavs, the city of Hastināpur

def: s.m. Ripe fruit, fruit; a berry.
n. f. S. पाण्डु pale. 1. A light-colored soil; a mixture of clay and sand. 2. Land dependant on rain-water ( bārānī ). pāṅ'ḍrī , n. f. Whitish, chalky earth.
s.f. See s.v. pan, 'water.' s.m. Drinking; drink, beverage
subs: v.n. To take a drink, to drink; to refresh oneself, make a repast (usually with jal, e.g. jal-pān kar-lo).

def: s. f. A waterfowl, the coot, the diver.
adj. Relating to the Pānḍavas or children of the Pānḍus (see next).
adj. & s.m. Yellowish-white, &c. ( = pānḍar); — a form of jaundice; white leprosy, vitiligo
subs: s.m. lit. 'The pale tree'; the plant Wrightia antidysenterica.

def: s.f. A species of hawk or falcon.
more: s. f. Name of a bird. (Falco).

def: s.m. Light-coloured or pale soil, mixed soil of clay and sand.
s.f. A patch or parcel of fruit or flower trees for transplantation; — a young flowering shrub or plant.
more: s. f. A young flowering shrub.

def: s.m. = pāṅk, q.v. s.m. Mud, mire; mud and sand left by inundations, alluvial deposit; slough, bog, quagmire.
s.m. Weaver's woof-frame.
s.m. Sea-salt, culinary salt obtained from sea-sand.
more: n. m. Culinary salt obtained from sea sand (as by the process in use on the sea coast of Bengal).
s. m. Culinary salt obtained from sea sand (as, by the process in use on the sea coast of Bengal).

def: adj. Crooked, deformed, &c., see pangu.
adj. (f. -ī), Watery, thin, weak; delicate, tender.
more: adj. 1. Weak; tender; delicate. 2. Thin; weak (hemp liquor). 3. Crooked.

def: s.m. A species of fish (see next).
more: s. f. Name of a fish. (Silurus sagittatus. Buch. ).

def: s.f. Line, row, &c. = pankti and pāṅt, qq.v.; a row of people sitting down at a meal; a company where seats are allotted according to rank; society, fellowship, brotherhood; body, band, company; tribe, caste
more: n. f. S. पंगत See پانتي 1. A tribe; caste. 2. A company where seats are allotted according to rank. 3. A row of people sitting down to a meal. paṅgat se bāhar , adv. Out of caste. pangat bīch likhat , Interlineation.
s. f. A row or line.

def: s.m. See s.v. pan, 'water.' s.m. Drinking; drink, beverage
more: s. m. A passage to a river, a stair or quay for drawing water.
subs: v.n. To take a drink, to drink; to refresh oneself, make a repast (usually with jal, e.g. jal-pān kar-lo).
ex: ... موہے پَنْگَھٹ پے نَنْد لال چھیڑ گَئیو رے ...
...Oh on the river quay, Nandlāl has teased me..
wety: From پَنـ (pan-, “water”) + گھات (ghāt, “bank”)

adj. = pangu, and panglā, q.v.; — s.m. A horse of a glossy or silvery-white colour; — also = paṅgutā, q.v.

def: s.m. Water (syn. jal); rain-water, rain ( = meṅh); semen, sperm; water or lustre (of a gem, &c.); lustre, sparkle, polish, brightness, beauty; spirit, mettle, blood, breed; character, reputation, honour; chastity, modesty, delicacy, sense of shame
more: (Padmāvat), n. f. S. पानीय, Pr. pāṇiam from पा to drink. 1. Water ( jal ). 1. Roṭī wahāṅ khāo, to pānī yahāṅ pīnā . Prov. saying. If you eat there, drink water here. (Use despatch). 2. Is ko wahāṅ māre, jahāṅ pānī na mile . Prov. Slay him where no water can be had. 3. Lāṭhī māre pānī judā nahīṅ hotā . Prov. Cudgel away, you cant divide the water. (Relations cannot be divided however they may quarrel). 4. Pānī āṅkhoṅ meṅ. Bon mot . Water in the eyes. (Drink your own tears). 2. Rain; rain water ( meṅh ). Aj baṛā pānī paṛā . 3. Semen. 4. Polish; gilding; sparkle ( āb , 2, 3). 5. Brightness; beauty. Ab to is par bhī pānī chaṛh gayā . 6. Breed; blood; mettle; spirit. Ṭāṅgan jān-dār thā, pānī aur khet kā bahut khūb: suīyoṅ ke chubhte hī sawār ko sarpaṭ le bhāgā . He was a spirited pony, of good blood and well bred, at a touch of the spur he flew. 7. Credit; reputation; honor ( ābrū , 1). Chor …

adj. 1. Thin as water; liquid. Sārā namak pānī ho gayā . 2. Cold as water; cold. Tavā to pānī ho rahā hai . 3. Watery; tasteless; insipid. Kharbūzā to pānī hai . 4. Lax; loose; not tight. Kankavvā pānī paṛā hai. pānī utarnā , v. n. 1. To have a cataract. Go haiṅ āṅkheṅ, pa sūjhtā kyā hai? Chashm nargis meṅ pānī utrā hai . Jurat. 2. To swell out (the scrotum); suffer from hydrocele ( āṅḍ baṛhnā ). pānī bujhānā , v. a. To thrust heated metal into water. pānī bharnā , v. a. 1. To draw or fetch water. Tū bhī rānī, maiṅ bhī rānī, kaun bharegā pānī? Prov. I'm a queen and you're a queen, who is to fetch the water then? (The more servants the less work). 2. To be humiliated or ashamed with a sense of inferiority. Woh to uske āge pānī bhartā hai. pānī pānā , v. n. E. To be delivered; to give birth to (jannā). pānī pānī honā , v. n. To perspire with shame ( āb āb honā ). Jis surāhī meṅ sāf pānī ha…

s. m. 1. Water. 2. Sperm. 3. Lustre, character, honour. پاني بهرنا pānī bharnā, To confess inferiority. پاني پڙنا pānī paṛnā, v. n. To rain. پاني پي پي کوسنا pānī pī pī kosnā, To curse excessively (i. e. till the throat, being dry, must be moistened by drinking). پاني جانا pānī jānā, v. n. 1. To be afflicted with the fluor albus. 2. To be disgraced. 3. To shed tears. پاني چلنا pānī chalnā, v. n. To be afflicted with the fluor albus. پاني دينا pānī denā, To offer a libation of water to satisfy the manes of a deceased person, after the corpse has been burnt. پاني سي پتلاکرنا pānī se patlā karnā, To abash, to put to shame. پاني کابلبلا pānī kā bulbulā (A bubble of water) indicates instability. پاني کرنا pānī karnā, v. a. 1. To abash. 2. To facilitate. پاني مرنا pānī marnā, v. n. 1. To dry up, to be evaporated. 2. To exhibit signs confirming a suspicion. پاني مين آگ لگانا pānī meṅ āg lagānā,…
subs: (-meṅ), To have a cataract (in the eye); to have a swollen scrotum, to suffer from hydrocele
v.n. To disinfect water by plunging heated iron into it
(meṅ), To fill vessels with water, to draw or fetch water; to be humiliated or ashamed; to feel a sense of inferiority (before or in the presence of); to confess inferiority
v.n. To perspire with shame; to be overwhelmed with shame
v.n. Water to run over or inundate (a field, &c.); (fig.) to be wasted, ruined, or destroyed
(-par), To impart lustre (to), to brighten, polish, gild; to plunge into, or submerge in, water; to dissipate, waste, destroy
(or pī-pī-ke) duʻā denā (-ko), To pray for one excessively (i.e. till the throat, becoming dry, must be moistened by drinking)
(or pī-pī-ke) kosnā (-ko), To curse excessively, to heap curses (upon)
pānī ćalnā, v.n. To shed tears; to be afflicted with the fluor albus; to be disgraced
(meṅ), v.n. To drop water; to drop water into the mouth of a dying person
v.n. To absorb water (a wound); to be pregnant
v.n. To give out water; to sweat

(-ko), To give water (to), to water (plants, &c.), to irrigate; to offer a libation of water to the manes of a deceased person aftor the corpse has been burnt
s.m. One whose duty it is to offer a libation of water to a deceased person; a son
adj. Thinner than water; watery; cheap, insignificant, worthless; easy
To make or render easy, to facilitate; to put to shame, to abash; to disgrace; to defeat
v.n. To make a bridge before there is water (to bridge); to devise plans against an improbable contingency
s.m. lit. 'Bubble of water'; anything unstable, weak or frail
v.n. To turn off water into another channel; to go or swim against the stream, to breast or stem the waves
v.n. To let out water, to make an outlet for water
adj. & s.m. Aquatic; an aquatic animal
v.n. To make water; — v.t. To convert into water, to liquefy, melt; to make easy, to facilitate; to abash; to dishearten
s.f. A bag or mass of water; anything very watery (as rice, herbs, &c.)
v.t. To give to the flood, to throw away, waste, squander; to sell very cheap or at a sacrifice
v.n. Water (of a place) to affect (one) injuriously; water to be unwholesome (e.g. pānī yahāṅ lagtā hai, 'The water here is unwholesome)
v.n. To draw water (a ship; = pānī toṛnā)
v.n. To run or flow (into); to leak; to be absorbed, be evaporated, to dry up; — to exhibit signs confirming a suspicion; to look suspicious
v.n. To revive a forgotten quarrel; to sow dissension, to set at loggerheads
v.n. To leak, drop, drip, ooze; semen to be discharged
v.n. To die instantly or suddenly (i.e. without having time to ask for water to cool one's dying lips); to be slain with a single stroke of a sword, &c.
To melt away, dissolve, liquefy; to melt, fail utterly (the heart); to become water, become watery or thin; to become loose or flaccid; to become cold as water; — to be very easy or simple.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi پانی (pānī), from Apabhramsa पाणिउ (pāṇiu), from Prakrit पाणीअ (pāṇīa), from Sanskrit पानीय (pānīya, “water, drink”).

To become thin; sloppy. pan-mār' , n. 1. Soil submerged by floods and rendered unfit for cultivation; low lands in which water lodges. 2. A crop spoiled through excess of rain.

more: adj. S. पञ्च 1. Five; cinque. 2. Seven years old (Horse dealers' idiom). panj āyat , n. The five chapters of the Quran which are read during the period of mourning for a person. panj-tan pāk , n. The five holy persons—Mahommad, Alī, Fatimā, Hasan, Husain.

adj. 1. Five. (Sans. पंच ). 2. Of seven years old (a horse). نئي پنج naye panj, Of five years old. ملي پنج male panj, Of ten years old. (These phrases are applied to horses only). پنجروزه panj-roza, The space of five days. پنج عيب شرعي panj-aﻌib-i-sharʼīﻌ. adj. Very vicious (possessed of five vices, i. e. 1. Robbery. 2. Adultery. 3. Gaming. 4. Drunkenness. 5. Falsehood). پنج آيت panj-āyat, The five chapters of the ḳurʼān, which are read during the mourning of a Musalmān. پنج تن پاک panj tan-i-pāk, Five holy persons (i. e. 1. Muhammad. 2. Alī. 3. Fātima. 4. Hasan. 5. Husain). پنج منزله panj-manzila, adj. Of five stories (a house, &c.).
subs: s.m. 'Five waters or rivers'; the land of five rivers, the Panjāb (situated to the north-west of Hindūstān)
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to the Panjāb; — an inhabitant or a native of the Panjāb; — s.f. The language spoken in the Panjāb

s.m. The five vices (of a horse); a very vicious horse; — adj. Very vicious; — panj ʻaib-i-sharaʻī, s.m. The five vices or sins, viz. robbery; adultery; gaming; drunkenness; falsehood; — adj. & s.m. Very vicious; — one who is very vicious, one who is addicted to the five vices
adj. & s.m. Five-fold; — the prayers appointed for five periods of the day
s.m. The five senses; the five stated daily prayers ( = panj-gāna); any collection of five things
panj-gosha, adj. Five-fold, quintuple; five cornered, pentangular

adj. Under five years old (a horse).

def: Panjshir (a province of Afghanistan)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پنجشیر (panjšir).

def: s.m. An aggregate of five (e.g. tīn panje pandrah, 'three fives are fifteen'); — also = panja, q.v.

s.m. An aggregate of five; the five of a suit of cards, or the five on a die; a cast or turn up of five with dice; — the hand with the fingers extended; claw, paw (of a tiger, &c.); clutch, grasp, possession, power; the five-fingered instrument of the religious mendicant; a sort of link or torch resembling the five fingers, or having five branches ( = panj-shāḵẖa); a hand made of ivory (to scratch the back with); the fore part of a foot or shoe; — cinq-foil
more: n. m. S. पञ्च five. 1. Five of anything. 2. The hand with the fingers extended; a claw; paw; cinque-foil. Panjā bharpūr, sab balā dūr! Benediction. Be your coffers ever full, and far off ills doleful. 3. The fore part of a foot or shoe. 4. Clutches; grasp; possession; power. panja phernā , v. n. To overcome; overpower. panja-dār . (Bot.) Palmate. panja karnā, yā laṛānā , v. n. To grapple by force of wrist, with the fingers interlocked. panja-kash , n. m. 1. An iron instrument resembling a hand, with which wrestlers exercise themselves by locking their fingers into those of the instrument. 2. A cake bearing the marks of the five fingers. panjoṅ ke bal chalnā , v. n. To walk on stilts; strut; mince; affect ( itrānā 1—3.) panje meṅ lānā , v. n. To get into one's clutches; bring under subjection. panja mārnā , v. n. To claw; snap at; snatch; pounce upon; dart at. panja-i-maryam ; H. hattā-jo…

s. m. 1. A hand with the five fingers extended, claw, clutch, paw, grasp. 2. A sort of link or torch resembling the five fingers, which is also called پنج شاخه panj-shāḵẖa. 3. A hand made of ivory, &c. to scratch the back with. پنجه پهيرنا panja phernā, To overcome, to overpower. پنجه کرنا panja karnā, A kind of wrestling, wherein the antagonists lock their fingers together. پنجه کش panja-kash, 1. s. m. An iron instrument resembling a hand, with which wrestlers exercise themselves by locking their fingers into those of the instrument. 2. (A kind of bread) bearing the marks of five fingers.
subs: (-kā), To twist or turn round the hand of an adversary by interlocking fingers with him; (fig.) to overcome, overpower
(-se), To interlock fingers with an adversary and endeavour to twist his hand or wrist
s.m. An iron instrument resembling a hand with which wrestlers exercise themselves by locking their fingers into those of the instrument; — a kind of bread bearing the marks of five fingers
(-se), To overcome an antagonist by twisting his hand or wrist, &c. ( = panja phernā); to carry off the palm
v.n. To walk on tip-toe; to walk on stilts, to strut
v.t. To claw; to snap or snatch at; — panja-i-maryam, s.f. A grass resembling a pair of claws interlocked
v.t. To get hold of, get into one's clutches; to bring under subjection
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پَنْجَه (panja). Doublet of پان٘چ (pā̃c, “five”).

def: n. m. S. पाद, Pr. pāam , P. pā . 1. Foot; leg; footmark. Hāthī ke pāoṅ meṅ sab kā pāoṅ hai . Prov. All feet in the elephant's feet. (The great man has numerous attendants). 2. Basis ( bunyād ); foundation; root ( jaṛ ). 3. Steadiness; decision ( istiqlāl ). Chor ke pāṅoṅ kahāṅ? Prov. The thief has no feet. (He never stands but flies). 4. Possession ( qabzā ); foothold; footing. 5. A part; portion; share. Zamīn meṅ unkā bhī pāoṅ hai . 6. Responsibility; liability; accountableness. Hamārā pāoṅ bīch meṅ hai . 7. Interference; meddling. pāṅoṅ uṭhā-kar chalnā, yā pāṅoṅ uṭhānā , v. n. To step out; walk quickly; mend one's pace; to take long strides. pāṅoṅ aṛānā , v. n. To interfere or meddle with. pāṅoṅ utarnā , v. n. To be dislocated (the foot). pāṅoṅ kā aṅgūṭhā , n. m. The great toe. pāṅoṅ ukhaṛnā , v. n. 1. To lose one's footing; to be carried down by the stream. Daryā meṅ nahāte nahāte pāṅoṅ ukhaṛ gaë 2. To run away; take flight; to be panic-stricken, routed. Fauj ke pāṅoṅ ukhaṛ gaë. pāoṅ bichalnā , v. n. To slip (the foot); lose one's balance; go wrong or astray. pāṅoṅ bhārī honā, do jī se honā, peṭ se honā , Wom; hamal, yā garabh se honā , v. n. To be pregnant, with child, in the family way; to carry. Soch iskā na ho gar mujh ko, to phir kisko ho? Raṅgīṅ. Jāntī tū nahīṅ kyā, pāṅoṅ hai bhārī, annā? Wom. Should I not anxious be, who then I pray? You know, nurse, I am in the family way. pāṅoṅ baṛhānā , v. n. 1. To take the lead; go ahead. 2. To overstep; exceed bounds; trespass; encroach upon. pāoṅ bīch meṅ honā , v. n. 1. To be responsible for. 2. To have a share in. pāṅoṅ pāṅoṅ, pairoṅ pairoṅ ; (a child) māoṅ māoṅ , adv. A foot; on foot; walking. Pāṅoṅ pāṅoṅ chal. pāṅoṅ pāṅoṅ chalnā, phirnā, yā ḍolnā , v. n. lit . to walk on foot. To be able to walk (a child). pāṅoṅ, paṛnā , W. v. n. To fall at one's feet. pāṅchwāṅ pāoṅ , n. m. lit . The fifth foot (of an animal). Membrum anīmale . 1. To fall at one's feet; prostrate oneself; entreat submissively. (Com. a phrase with men, an act with Hin. Wom). 1. Nā pāṅoṅ paṛ kisī ke tū, aë dil, na joṛ hāth, Do hāth-wāle jitne haiṅ, in sab se moṛ hāth. Us se hī māṅg jiske haiṅ ab sau karoṛ hāth . Nazīr. Fall not on any's feet, nor stand with folded hands, Beg not, O heart, of any creature with two hands, Ask thou of Him alone who hath ten million hands. 2. Jiṭhānī jī, pairoṅ paṛūṅ! Wom. My husband's eldest brother's wife, I prostrate myself before your feet! (A salutation). 2. To beseech; implore; entreat; supplicate. Pāṅoṅ pasārnā , v. n. lit . To stretch one's legs. 1. To die; expire; breathe one's last. Lālā jī ke pāṅoṅ pasārte hī ghar birān ho gayā . No sooner were the Lalā's legs stretched in death than ruin came on the house. 2. To stretch one's legs full length; lie comfortably. Jitnī chādar dekhye, utne pāṅoṅ pasārye . Prov. Stretch your feet according to your coverlet. (Cut your coat according to your cloth). pāṅoṅ pakaṛnā , v. n. To hold one's feet; fall at one's feet ( pāṅoṅ par girnā ) (in the act of supplication, or as a token of reverence). pāṅoṇ pūjnā, chūmnā, yā dho dho-ke pīnā , v. n. To worship or kiss one's feet, or drink the water after washing the feet (of one held in reverence); to do honor or reverence to. pāoṅ phaṭnā , v. n. To have chilblains. pāṅoṅ phūlnā , v. n. 1. To be tired, weary, fatigued. 2. To be perplexed, embarrassed, at one's wits end. Gāṛī ko dekh-ke, lārī ke pāṅoṅ phūlte haiṅ! Prov. The pampered one a carriage sees—with walking, oh! so tired she is! pāoṅ phernā , v. n. Wom. To visit her parent's or other house after childbirth. pāṅoṅ phailānā , v. n. See pāoṅ pasārnā . To raise one's demands; to be very pressing; to insist upon; make a long arm. pāṅoṅ phailā-ke sonā , v. n. lit . To sleep with one's legs extended. To sleep in security; to be free from care; to be perfectly contended and happy. pāṅoṅ pe pāṅoṅ rakhnā , v. n. 1. To sit crosslegged. pāṅoṅ pe pāṅoṅ dhar-ke baiṭhnā manhūs hai . Wom. It is unlucky to sit with one leg on the other. 2. To imitate or copy one; to follow an example. pāṅoṅ pīṭnā , v. n. 1. To throw about one's legs (in the agonies of death); to die hard. 2. To stamp with impatience, or anger. 3. To struggle in vain against difficulties; to beat the air. Sohan Lāl. Na pauṅhcheṅ, agar pāṅoṅ pīṭeṅ hazār, Yeh sunnā, samajhnā, yeh soch aur bīchār . feet. pāṅoṅ tale kī chiūṅṭī , n. f. lit . An ant under one's feet. A helpless, miserable creature. pāṅoṅ tale yā nīche kī maṭṭī , n. f. lit . The dust under one's feet. Dust from under the feet of one who had cast an evil eye on another, collected and thrown with some five or seven chillies into the fire. (Wom. and Conjurors). pāṅoṅ tale kī matṭī nikalnā, yā saraknā , v. n. To feel as though the ground was slipping from under one's feet; to be stunned, stupefied; to feel one's head swim. pāṅoṅ tale malnā , v. n. To trample under foot; to crush. pāṅoṅ toṛnā , v. n. lit . To break one's legs in vain. To be marched or trotted about in vain. pāṅoṅ jam'nā, gaṛnā, yā ṭiknā , v. n. To stand firmly; to have a good footing. pāṅoṅ chhūṭnā , A flow of blood after menstruation; menorrhagia. Tū mūṅh kī pīk kā de likh-ke ek jo tāvīz, To pāṅoṅ chhūṭā ho jiskā wahīṅ pe jāve tham . Rangīṅ If with pān spittle you a magic charm would write, The excessive flow of menstruation you'd check quite. pāoṅ jamānā , v. a. To be firm on one's feet; to plant oneself; to settle down; get a footing or foothold. pāṅoṅ dharnā, yā rakhnā , v. n. 1. To set foot on; step in; come in; enter; land. Ūpar chaṛhe, na bhūīṅ gire, dhartī dhare na pāṅoṅ. Chand, suraj ke ojhal rahe, wākā kyā hai nāṅoṅ? Riddle. Ans. Gūlar kā kīṛā . Nor climbs nor falls, nor plants his feet upon the ground, From sun and moon both hidden: this riddle now expound. 2. To go or step forward; advance; proceed. 3. To begin; commence; adopt; take up. pāoṅ-dauṛī , n. f. Endeavour; exertion; effort. Roṭī qismat kī, huqqā pāoṅ-dauṛī kā! Prov. Bread's given in the lot, a smoke with labor's got! pāṅoṅ dho-dho-ke pīnā , v. n. lit . To drink the water in which one's feet have been washed. To show affection or respect; to do honor to. pāṅoṅ zamīn par na rakhnā . More com. zamīn par pāṅoṅ na rakhnā , v. n. To walk on stilts ( panjoṅ ke bal chalnā ); walk on eggs; walk like a swell; show off ( itrānā ). pāṅoṅ kī uṅglī , n. f. A toe. pāṅoṅ ḍignā , v. n. 1. To slip (the legs) ( phisalnā ); lose one's footing. 2. To give way (a foundation, an army). pāṅoṅ sonā , v. n. To be numb; to go to sleep (a foot). pāṅoṅ gor meṅ laṭkānā . More com. gor meṅ pāṅoṅ laṭkānā , Mah. v. n. To have one foot in the grave; to become old; to be advanced in years. pāṅoṅ ghis'nā , v. n. 1. To be worn (the skin of one's feet). Sandal nigoṛī, tūjh ko bhī yeh din lage, cheh khush! Ghiste tumhāre pāoṅ haiṅ chalte makān tak! Wom. Jan Sahab. Sandal, you minx, is your day too then come? how fine! You could n't walk home now—your skin's so very fine! 2. To be always on the trot or tramp; to undergo useless labor. dabe pāṅoṅ ānā , v. n. To come on one quietly, slily, unperceived (as a cat or a spy). pāṅoṅ laṛkhaṛānā, tharrānā, laraznā, yā ḍigmigānā , v. n. To tremble; shake; quake; totter (one's feet). Jhūm jhūm aise bādal āne lage, Pāṅoṅ tobā ke laṛkharāne lage . Zauq. The rain clouds gathering are and lowering so, My vow of abstinence is tottering so. pāṅoṅ se pāṅoṅ bāṅdhnā . v. n. lit . To tie one's feet to your own. 1. To keep one a close prisoner; watch one closely. 2. To vie with; to try to tire one out. pāṅoṅ lagnā , v. n. See pāṅoṅ paṛnā. Sās jī se kahiyo, 'terī paīyāṅ lāgūṅ', Nanadi jī se kahiyo rī, 'salām .' Song. Barsātī. Say to my mother-in-law, 'I lie before her feet,' My husband's sister with ' salām ' I also greet. pāoṅ meṅ chakkar honā , v. n. To be always wandering about. pāṅoṅ meṅ meṅhdī lagānā , v. a. lit . To apply henna to the feet; hence, to be unable to walk or set foot on the ground. pāṅoṅ nikālnā , v. n. lit . To shew one's feet. 1. To shew the cloven hoof; to show one's teeth. 2. To betray one's evil disposition; begin one's pranks. Ab to is laṛke ne bhī pāṅoṅ nikāle . 3. To withdraw from; get out of (a mess); save oneself. Ham ne to apnā pāṅoṅ nikāl līyā, ab woh jāne. phaṭe meṅ pāoṅ denā , v. n. To stand in the breach; risk one's life for another. phūṅk phūṅk ke pāoṅ rakhnā , v. n. To tread softly as on cinders; to be very cautious. kāṭ meṅ pāoṅ denā , v. a. To put one in the stocks. zamīn pāṅoṅ lagnā , v. n. To be used or accustomed to a long or tedious road. apne pāṅoṅ meṅ āp kulhāṛī mārnā , v. a. To cut one's own feet. See under apnā .
